channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Anti-Israel protesters pitch encampment outside Jewish Democrat's Ohio home
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Masked anti-Israel demonstrators escalated their harassment of a Jewish congressman on the eve of Monday’s anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, pitching an encampment outside Ohio Democratic Rep. Greg Landsman ’s Cincinnati residence.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal from Texas officer convicted in killing of woman through her window
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court won't hear an appeal from a former Texas police officer convicted in the death of a woman who was shot through a window of her home. The justices did not detail their reasoning, as is typical, and none publicly dissented.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tren de Aragua members arrested in Texas raid
item.description: The raid saw multiple Tren de Aragua members arrested after complaints alleging human trafficking and narcotic violations at the complex.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Miss Teen Rodeo Kansas, 19, killed in car crash; teammates injured
item.description: Emma Brungardt, 19, was killed in a single-vehicle crash that left several of her teammates injured on Friday in rural Thomas County, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips, overtime, Social Security: A look at Donald Trump's no-tax pledges and what they might cost
item.description: Donald Trump has pledged to end taxes on everything from tips to Social Security and overtime pay if he's elected to the White House again. But he hasn't detailed how he would fund those ideas and avoid creating a huge budget shortfall, beyond arguing he will usher in an economic boom.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: New Jersey high school allegedly banned yellow ribbons honoring Israeli hostages: 'Deeply offensive'
item.description: Yellow ribbons meant to honor Israeli hostages abducted by Hamas were allegedly banned at a club fair, which critics called "deeply offensive" and "blatant antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelan man uses love for cooking to make a home in Texas
item.description: Saul Aguero, a Venezuelan native who moved across the world to make Austin his home, shares his love for the kitchen with the community.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to save big on Prime Day by loading your carts now
item.description: Amazon's Prime Big Deal Days is almost here. Here's how to make sure you're getting the best deal possible during the event.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Palmer, Fox 5

item.title: Affluent Virginia suburb rocked by love triangle murders as prosecutors highlight key evidence
item.description: Virginia prosecutors say blood-spatter analysis led to the arrest of Brendan Banfield in connection with the 2023 murders of his wife and an unknown man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fact-checking scam claims about Hurricane Helene
item.description: Scam artists, bad-faith actors and those with political agendas are spreading falsehoods, says a White House statement.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dodgers' Kershaw, Guardians' Lively, Tigers' Mize and Yankees' Stroman are left off ALDS rosters
item.description: Pitchers Clayton Kershaw and Joe Kelly were left off the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for their NL Division Series against the San Diego Padres that started Saturday. San Diego added right-handers Martín Pérez and Alek Jacob, who were skipped from the Wild Card Series roster against Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ardent Trump supporters weigh in on safety at historic Pennsylvania rally
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service has "heightened" the security at the Trump rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, with rally goers saying that they felt safe attending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Milton forms in the Gulf, NHC says
item.description: Tropical Depression Fourteen has strengthened into Tropical Storm Milton, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump's return to Butler, Pennsylvania, site of first assassination attempt, is 'gutsy,' supporters say
item.description: Supporters praised former President Trump for returning to Butler, Pennsylvania following the July assassination attempt, saying that it was "courageous" and "gutsy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Several Southern California cities home to the nation's worst drivers, but others are home to the best
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court's election-season term that begins Monday, with the prospect of the court's intervention in voting disputes lurking in the background.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: TSA detects shotgun shell containing meth hidden in traveler's groin area at Pennsylvania airport
item.description: A traveler attempting to hide a prohibited substance inside a prohibited item — and then tucking them into his groin area — was quickly caught by TSA officers this week.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, Fox 5

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: NYC accelerates school leadership change as investigations swirl around mayor’s indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City is speeding up its switch to a new schools chief as indicted Mayor Eric Adams faces mounting pressure to bring stability to a city government that has been roiled by searches, subpoenas and resignations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coach praises Tim Walz's son for helping protect other kids after shooting
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — When the issue of gun violence came up at the vice presidential debate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz had a personal story to tell: His teenage son, Gus, had been at a sports center last year when a shooting took place.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4
